text: Hello from Munich and welcome to This is my Architecture. Today I'm joined by Christian
from BASF Digital Farming. Hi Christian. Hey David, thanks for having me. Sure. Christian,
can you tell me about BASF Digital Farming? Sure. At BASF Digital Farming we develop soft
and hardware solutions for farmers to help feed the world more sustainably and improve farmers
bottom line. Okay great. So what's the solution we're going to talk about today? We are talking
about a nifty little tool that we call Examt for cross-account model deployment that has been
first developed for Xavio Scouting, our image recognition app to help you identify diseases,
weeds and many more. It has been downloaded by more than 6.5 million users.
From over 100 countries. Okay that's quite the scale. Yes. And when you say cross-account model
deployment right, we're talking about machine learning. So you use machine learning to build
these applications for your customers. So let's dive into the architecture and talk first about
how you build models. So can you tell me about that process? Yeah. Our data scientists use Sage
maker to create ML models. These models are then basically done in automated pipelines that
then register a model into SageMaker model registry. Okay. And from there, right, so you, your main
challenge was I assume like productionizing these models, right? And we have here three different
accounts, which is the data science account, the QA account, the product account. So what were the
challenges you faced there trying to productionize these? Yeah. So once we had a validated model,
the question was how can we actually deploy such models across the different accounts from a data
science to a QA to a production account, for example, for Scouting. And we wanted to have a reliable
mechanism to do the cross account model deployment. And that's where we ended up developing EXAMT.
Okay. EXAMT, which stands for cross account model deployment, right? Yeah. So can you go a little
bit more into detail? What exactly is EXAMT and what does it do? EXAMT is a serverless application that is
deployed by our admins. It basically updates and provisions resources so that you can transition a
SageMaker model through different stages. And it heavily relies on API gateways that we call deployment
APIs then. Okay. And step functions. So once you invoke the deployment API, the step function will then
fetch the model artifacts and deploy through the different accounts. Okay, got it. Okay, so workflow-wise,
right? The data scientist registers the model in the model registry. Then what happens next? Yeah. So
data scientists come and go to the model registry. They approve the models in registry, which then
basically notifies our ML engineers responsible for operationalization in all of our production models.
Okay. So this ML engineer then invokes the deployment API of EXAMT, which then basically fetches
the model artifact from here and deploys it to the SageMaker endpoint in the QA account first.
Okay, awesome. And EXAMT basically takes care of that all in the background, right? It's not
additional. Yeah. Okay. So in the QA account, I assume you do like additional testing for load and
accuracy, right? That's what we do. So yeah, the usual suspects of load tests are performed. Once these are done,
the ML engineer invokes EXAMT again, but now in the production account, which then fetches the model
artifact from the QA account and deploys it to the PROT account. All right. And from there,
it's basically available to the clients we have here on the bottom, right? So how do they get access to
the predictions that are served in the production endpoint? Yeah. So there's a REST interface between our
SageMaker model endpoint and the scouting production account. So once a farmer takes a photo with our
scouting app, this image is then posted against the SageMaker endpoint and the prediction is created
that is fed back to the scouting middleware, which is then also served to our customers.
Okay, great. Yeah. I really like the way you productionized machine learning here with
using this cross-account model deployment framework. I would like to ask, do you want to share a few
pieces of information around the journey or the evolution of how you got where you are today?
Yeah. So we came from a very manual and inefficient process of deploying models through the different
accounts. And EXAMM just gives us an ability to increase the resilience and reliability of this process
through well-defined APIs. Got it. Yeah. Thanks so much, Christian, for sharing that. Thanks for having me.
Sure. Well, thank you for watching. This is more architecture.
